🌟 AItzik's TOP RECOMMENDATIONS

━━ FORD RANGER ━━
💡 Why this model: The Ranger is explicitly a 4x4 pick-up with model years covering 2019 and later (data range 2011–2025). The retrieved pricing guidance shows many used examples in accessible ranges (early facelifted/used examples from around £8,000 up to facelifted examples from ~£17,000 and Euro 6 examples from ~£19,000; near-new low-mileage examples around £23,000). The Ranger offers strong towing (up to 3500kg with the correct axle) and a range of cab styles (including double cab for everyday practicality), making it a versatile choice for a used pickup. The data also notes reasonable used availability and trims (Limited recommended) and practical servicing/fixed-price options for older 2.2-litre models, supporting ownership cost predictability.

🌟 AItzik's TOP RECOMMENDATIONS

━━ HYUNDAI IONIQ ━━
💡 Why this model: The Ioniq is offered as a fully electric model in the 2017-2022 range (meets the 2016+ year requirement). The retrieved data explicitly states the fully electric Ioniq is available and lists used prices starting around £7,000, which is well within the user's budget ceiling as stated in the data. The Ioniq EV also has useful official ranges cited (around 174–194 miles for later EVs) and strong value/kit levels, making it a practical, budget-friendly electric choice despite a noted comment about possible reliability concerns.
